content.sanskrit: अन्यच्छ्रेयोऽन्यदुतैव प्रेयस्ते उभे नानार्थे पुरुषं सिनीतः । तयोः श्रेय आददानस्य साधु भवति हीयतेऽर्थाद्य उ प्रेयो वृणीते ॥
content.transliteration: anyachchreyo'nyadutaiva preyaste ubhe nānārthe puruṣaṁ sinītaḥ | tayoḥ śreya ādadānasya sādhu bhavati hīyate'rthādya u preyo vṛṇīte ||
content.text: The good (Shreya) and the pleasant (Preya) are different. Both of these bind a person. It goes well with him who clings to the good, but he who chooses the pleasant misses the true end of life.

content.sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत । क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति ॥
content.transliteration: uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpya varānnibodhata | kṣurasya dhārā niśitā duratyayā durgaṁ pathastatkavayo vadanti ||
content.text: Arise! Awake! Approach the great teachers and learn from them. The path is as difficult to tread as a razor's edge, so say the wise.
